title: copilot freshness --json
description: The cheap poll target: a single-SHA staleness check, not a full update. CORRECTED 2026-07-07 (WS-A slice 3, cc owner): current_lock_sha/latest_lock_sha/stale widened to nullable and a required `offline` field added -- see the $comment on `stale` for why the original non-nullable shape could not honestly represent 'could not check'.

latest_lock_sha.description: The tier's published lock-pointer ref target. Null when it could not be fetched (offline/unreachable) -- see `offline`.

current_lock_sha.description: Fingerprint of the local resolved lock state. Null when no local lock exists yet (first run).

stale.description: True when current_lock_sha lags latest_lock_sha; false when they match; null when freshness could not be determined (offline, or no local lock yet).
stale.$comment: CORRECTED 2026-07-07 (WS-A slice 3): the original non-nullable boolean forced the CLI to fabricate a value whenever either SHA was unknown -- e.g. reporting `false` ("up to date") while offline, which is a false-Healthy-shaped bug (mirrors doctor.schema.json's 'a false Healthy is impossible' invariant). `stale: null` is the honest third state; a consumer MUST treat null as unknown, never as `false`. The stale<=>SHA-inequality invariant still cannot be cross-field-encoded in JSON Schema; the CLI computes it and the app parses it (invariant #1).

offline.description: True when a remote lock-pointer check was attempted and failed/was unreachable. False both when the check succeeded AND when no tier source was configured to check in the first place (distinguish the latter via current_lock_sha/latest_lock_sha both null with offline:false).

layers.description: OPTIONAL, ADDITIVE (WS-A update-slice, cc owner): per-layer freshness breakdown, one entry per manifest layer, alongside (never replacing) the top-level single-SHA fields above. Omitted entirely when the CLI was not asked to compute it -- the top-level fields remain the cheap-poll default; this array is populated only by the opt-in per-layer build path (`cc/core/ecosystem/freshness.py`'s `build_per_layer_freshness()`), not by every `freshness --json` invocation.

id.description: The manifest layer id (four-tier-topology.md §4 `layers[].id`) this entry reports on.

current.description: Fingerprint of this layer's own local mirror lock state. Null when no local mirror exists yet for this layer.

latest.description: This layer's own published lock-pointer ref target. Null when it could not be fetched (offline/unreachable), or when the layer has no remote source to poll.

stale.description: Same honesty rule as the top-level `stale`, scoped to this one layer: null unless both `current` and `latest` are known -- never coerced to false.

offline.description: Same rule as the top-level `offline`, scoped to this one layer: true only when this layer has a remote source AND the poll came back unknown.
